name: Puigsacalm and Puig dels Llops - Circular from Collado de Bracons
name_original: Puigsacalm y Puig dels Llops - Circular desde el Collado de Bracons
region_intro: Hiking around Sant Pere De Torelló offers a varied landscape, situated at the confluence of the Ges and Fornès rivers and at the foot of the Bellmunt mountain range. The terrain transitions from cereal fields and rain-eroded slopes of the Vic Plain to extensive mountain holm oak, oak, and beech forests, including the Fageda de la Grevolosa. Prominent features include the Serra de Bellmunt and the Puigsacalm, providing diverse natural environments for outdoor enthusiasts. Approximately 70% of the area is covered by forests and pastures. Best hiking trails around Sant Pere De Torelló The most popular hiking route is Puigsacalm and Puig dels Llops - Circular from Collado de Bracons, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This route ascends to the highest point of the Serralada Transversal, offering excellent panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Forest of La Grevolosa - circular, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This trail leads through an ancient beech forest, known for its vibrant colors, especially in autumn. Local hikers also love the Vidrà Mill Waterfall – Salgueda Bridge loop from Bellmunt, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail leading through diverse scenery near the Bellmunt mountain, often completed in about 3 hours 25 minutes. Hiking around Sant Pere De Torelló is defined by mountain ranges like Serra de Bellmunt and Puigsacalm, extensive holm oak and beech forests, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Sant Pere De Torelló are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 3,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Sant Pere De Torelló's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Sant Pere de Torelló?
A: There are over 45 hiking routes around Sant Pere de Torelló, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 8 easy trails, 26 moderate routes, and 12 more challenging options, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Sant Pere de Torelló?
A: The trails in Sant Pere de Torelló are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 3,400 hikers have explored the area, often praising the varied landscapes, from river valleys to mountain peaks, and the well-preserved natural environment.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Sant Pere de Torelló?
A: Yes, Sant Pere de Torelló offers 8 easy trails perfect for beginners or families. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976978719/forest-of-la-grevolosa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest of La Grevolosa - circular&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 5.8 km walk through an ancient beech forest, particularly stunning in autumn.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the hiking trails in this region?
A: The terrain around Sant Pere de Torelló is highly varied. You'll find gentle paths through cereal fields and rain-eroded slopes closer to the plain, transitioning to extensive mountain holm oak, oak, and beech forests in higher elevations. River valleys also offer walks through riparian forests, past old spas and Romanesque bridges.

Q: Are there any hikes with impressive viewpoints in the area?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its panoramic views. The ascent to the Bellmunt Sanctuary, for instance, offers spectacular 360-degree vistas of the Pyrenees, Montseny Massif, and the Vic Plain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926174269/puigsacalm-and-puig-dels-llops-circular-from-collado-de-bracons" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puigsacalm and Puig dels Llops - Circular from Collado de Bracons&lt;/a&gt; route also leads to the highest point of the Serralada Transversal, providing excellent views.

Q: Can I find trails that feature waterfalls or unique natural formations?
A: Yes, the area has several natural attractions. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/380370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Foradada Waterfall and Mill&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful spot to visit. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/741468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Bufadors de Beví&lt;/a&gt; offers unique geological formations to discover.

Q: Are there any notable forests or natural areas to explore?
A: The Fageda de la Grevolosa is a particularly stunning ancient beech forest, especially vibrant in autumn. The region is also rich in extensive mountain holm oak and oak forests, covering approximately 70% of the area, providing lush natural environments for hiking.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Sant Pere de Torelló?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976978719/forest-of-la-grevolosa-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forest of La Grevolosa - circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926174269/puigsacalm-and-puig-dels-llops-circular-from-collado-de-bracons" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puigsacalm and Puig dels Llops - Circular from Collado de Bracons&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sant Pere de Torelló?
A: The area is beautiful year-round, but autumn is particularly recommended for hiking, especially to experience the vibrant colors of the Fageda de la Grevolosa beech forest. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and blooming wildflowers.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails that pass through Sant Pere de Torelló?
A: Yes, the long-distance trails GR-151 (Camí Oliba) and GR-3 both pass through the municipality. These trails offer segments for hikers, including sections with great viewpoints of the valley and rocky paths, allowing for longer excursions.

Q: What are some of the key landmarks or attractions I can see while hiking?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can encounter cultural and historical landmarks. The Bellmunt Sanctuary, perched atop Bellmunt mountain, is a significant point of interest. Routes along the Ges river also pass by old spas, Romanesque bridges, and various natural springs.
tips: My 8 year old retriever had difficulties with certain segments here and needed help. Small kids will also have problems here.

The Puigsacalm is a mountain of 1,515 meters that is located in the municipality of Vall d'en Bas in la Garrotxa. It is the highest point of the Serralada Transversal, an emblematic mountain in the hiking world, with excellent views of the peaks and mountains of the Principality.
This peak is included in the list of the 100 peaks of the F.E.E.C. and also to that of the 100 most emblematic peaks of Catalonia.
If the most used route to climb it is from the Collada de Bracons, we can design a handful of itineraries which one is better.

Neighboring mountain of Puigsacalm with a very characteristic shape that makes it attractive to climb. The views from the top, like those of Puigscalam, are a perfect viewpoint of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park.
